metadata.title: Lookout-SmishingAlert-UserNotify
metadata.description: When a critical or high severity smishing or phishing incident is created in Microsoft Sentinel by Lookout, this playbook enriches the incident with a comment documenting the phishing campaign context, attack intelligence, and recommended analyst next steps.
prerequisites: 1. The playbook managed identity must be granted the Microsoft Sentinel Responder role on the Log Analytics workspace.
postDeployment: 1. Assign the Microsoft Sentinel Responder role to the playbook managed identity on your Log Analytics workspace.
2. In Microsoft Sentinel, create an Automation Rule that runs this playbook when incidents are created by the 'Lookout - Critical Smishing and Phishing Alerts (v2)' analytic rule.

metadata.description: Name for the Logic App playbook resource.

body.message:
🤖 Lookout Smishing/Phishing Alert — Automated Response Summary

Attack Intelligence
• Alert Type: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['SmishAlertType']?[0]}
• Threat Category: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['ThreatCategory']?[0]}
• Severity: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['SmishSeverity']?[0]}
• Risk Score: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['AlertRiskScore']?[0]}/10
• Impersonation Risk: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['ImpersonationRisk']?[0]}
• Campaign Indicators: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['CampaignIndicators']?[0]}

Device Context
• Platform: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['DevicePlatform']?[0]}
• Security Status: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['DeviceSecStatus']?[0]}
• Device Risk Level: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['customDetails']?['DeviceRiskLevel']?[0]}

Actions Taken
✅ Incident enriched with Lookout smishing/phishing campaign intelligence

Recommended Next Steps
1. Contact device owner to confirm they have NOT clicked any suspicious links
2. If credentials were entered on a phishing site, initiate password reset immediately
3. Review CampaignIndicators — if 'Targeted Campaign' or 'Sophisticated Attack', check for other affected devices
4. Check if the impersonated entity (e.g., executive, IT support) needs to be notified
5. Preserve any phishing URLs or message content as evidence before blocking
6. Consider blocking sender number/domain at the carrier or email gateway level
